pi_items.source: task.json and setup_task.sh confirm this filename is used throughout
export_result.sh confirms PNG export to this filename

pi_items.metadata_value: 11 states: CLOSED, LISTEN, SYN_SENT, SYN_RECEIVED, ESTABLISHED, FIN_WAIT_1, FIN_WAIT_2, CLOSE_WAIT, CLOSING, LAST_ACK, TIME_WAIT
pi_items.verified_value: RFC 793 and RFC 9293 define exactly these 11 TCP states (using hyphens in RFC: SYN-SENT, etc.). Task uses underscores per naming convention.
pi_items.source: RFC 9293 confirmed via web search; setup_task.sh reference file lists all 11 states from RFC 793/9293

pi_items.verified_value: ["SYN", "ACK", "FIN", "RST", "close", "open", "timeout"] - all keywords found in TCP state transitions
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh tcp_states_rfc793.txt shows transitions labeled with these events (e.g., 'Send SYN', 'Recv ACK', 'Recv FIN', 'Close', 'Passive Open', 'Timeout=2MSL')

pi_items.verified_value: 9 is a reasonable minimum threshold; task requires all 11 but scorer accepts 9+
pi_items.source: verifier.py scoring logic: 9+ states = 20pts, 6-8 states = 12pts, 3-5 states = 5pts

pi_items.verified_value: RFC 793/9293 defines 19 explicit state transitions; metadata minimum of 14 covers main establishment/teardown paths
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lists 19 transitions in tcp_states_rfc793.txt; verifier.py scoring: 14+ edges = 15pts, 8-13 = 8pts

pi_items.verified_value: 2 pages required: Page 1 'Complete State Machine' and Page 2 'Happy Path'
pi_items.source: task.json description section explicitly requires 2 pages; verifier.py checks for 2+ pages = 10pts

pi_items.verified_value: RFC 793 (original TCP standard from 1981) and RFC 9293 (2022 revision) both define same 11-state machine
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed RFC 9293 at datatracker.ietf.org and rfc-editor.org; setup_task.sh explicitly references RFC 793/RFC 9293

pi_items.verified_value: draw.io Desktop 26.0.9 released January 27, 2025 for Linux (amd64)
pi_items.source: GitHub releases and Filepuma confirm v26.0.9 exists; install_drawio.sh specifies this version

privileged_info_summary: This task is based on RFC 793/RFC 9293 TCP state machine specification. The 11 required states (CLOSED, LISTEN, SYN_SENT, SYN_RECEIVED, ESTABLISHED, FIN_WAIT_1, FIN_WAIT_2, CLOSE_WAIT, CLOSING, LAST_ACK, TIME_WAIT) are the authoritative TCP states from the Transmission Control Protocol standard. The data is not synthetic—it is derived from IETF RFC standards. The task requires drawing a complete state machine diagram with 19 possible transitions, with a minimum threshold of 14 transitions to score points. The reference file provided in setup matches RFC 793/9293 specifications. draw.io Desktop version 26.0.9 (January 27, 2025) is the specified tool.
